bug_report.summary: Tab-strip control near “Split View” tab (close/indicator area) renders differently after change (possible misrender/hover/clip)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the app to the main interface (as in the scenario, after skipping the setup wizard).
Observe the tab strip at the top of the editor, specifically the right side of the “Split View” tab (close/controls region).
expected_behaviors: Tab close/control area should render consistently and correctly (icon/shape/position not changing unexpectedly between builds).
actual_behaviors: A small visual change is present in the “Split View” tab’s close/controls region (bbox ~[749,171]-[772,188]), indicating the control/icon/state is rendered differently after the code change. This appears unrelated to the intended table editor UX improvements.

bug_report.summary: Backspace at beginning of table cell moves caret unexpectedly (caret position jumps)
steps_to_reproduce: Insert a pipe table with 2 columns and 3 total rows (header + 2 body rows).
Click in the first body cell (row 1, col 1) and type “alpha”.
Place the caret immediately before the “a” in “alpha”.
expected_behaviors: Backspace at the start of a table cell should not move the caret to a different position (and should not cross/delete table boundaries).
Caret position indicators (line/column) should remain consistent with being at the start of the cell content.
actual_behaviors: After the code change, the caret position indicators change (e.g., from ~4:3 to ~4:9 as shown in the top-right/status bar), indicating the caret jumped to a different location even though the cell text remains “alpha”.

bug_report.summary: Multiline paste sanitization drops an entire line of text instead of replacing newline with a space
steps_to_reproduce: Click into the first body cell (row 1, col 1).

Paste multiline text:
First line of pasted text.
Second line should become a space.
Third line ends here.
expected_behaviors: All pasted text remains present, with line breaks sanitized (e.g., converted into spaces) so content becomes a single-line string in the cell: “First line of pasted text. Second line should become a space. Third line ends here.”
actual_behaviors: The middle line text (“Second line should become a space.”) is missing after paste; only the first and third lines remain visible in the cell (diff region 3 ~[898,515]-[1164,733]).

bug_report.summary: After clicking “+ column”, the horizontal bar/overlay beneath the table renders inconsistently (filled gray vs empty/outlined)
steps_to_reproduce: Insert a 2-column pipe table with a header row and 2 body rows.
Click into the first body cell and enter some text (e.g., “kiwi”).
Click the “+ column” control above the table headers to add a column.
expected_behaviors: Adding a column should only change the table structure and controls; no unexpected overlay/blank bar should appear below the table, and any table-editor UI elements should render consistently.
actual_behaviors: A wide horizontal bar/overlay area below the table changes appearance after the code change (diff at bbox ~[894,667]-[1223,690]): before shows a filled gray bar, after shows an empty/outlined bar, suggesting a rendering/style or focus-state issue triggered by adding a column.
